headline: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky Passed on OpenAI — Now He’s Quietly Building His Own AI Company
summary: The Airbnb founder has said AI chatbots aren't robust enough for the travel industry. So he's building a company to deliver what he needs.

headline: 1 of Wall Street’s Favorite Stocks to Research Further and 2 That Underwhelm
summary: Wall Street is overwhelmingly bullish on the stocks in this article, with price targets suggesting significant upside potential. However, it’s worth remembering that analysts rarely issue sell ratings, partly because their firms often seek other business from the same companies they cover.

headline: AI Travel Assistant And Asian Demand Shape Booking Holdings Outlook
summary: Priceline has introduced Penny, a next generation AI powered travel assistant designed to help customers plan and book trips more efficiently. Agoda has released new research highlighting the growing influence of Asian travelers on global travel patterns and demand. Both developments relate to Booking Holdings' major platforms and point to shifts in customer behavior and product capabilities across its portfolio. Booking Holdings (NasdaqGS:BKNG), which last closed at $167.49, is rolling out...

headline: Agoda Unveils Asia's Hidden Nature Retreats for 2026
summary: In 2026, the allure of nature therapy and wellness is captivating travelers more than ever. As people increasingly seek to escape the hustle and bustle of daily life, digital travel platform Agoda notes a rising trend towards destinations that offer serene landscapes and rejuvenating experiences.

headline: 520 Is No Longer Just for Couples! Agoda Reveals Busan and Chiayi as Emerging Favorites Among Solo Travelers
summary: "520" (May 20), widely recognized in Taiwan as an unofficial "Love Day" due to its similar pronunciation to "I love you" in Mandarin, has evolved beyond a celebration for couples. Today, more Taiwanese are embracing solo travel as a form of self-reward and personal ritual. In response to this trend, digital travel platform Agoda has observed growing interest in solo travel among Taiwanese travelers. According to Agoda data, searches made by Taiwanese solo travelers increased by 16% year-on-year
